title: Signed Language Writing Critical Review Series Overview
subtitle: Scope, method, structure, reading paths, and future direction for the review series
summary: Layer-based review of signed-language writing, notation, encoding, standards, and representation proposals.
synopsis: The review series asks what level of the signed-writing problem each source actually solves. Its method, structure, main review set, reading paths, recurring critique categories, and future direction keep useful contributions separate from unresolved writing, encoding, rendering, and infrastructure gaps.
cover_image_alt: A scholarly review desk with layered source packets, SignWriting-like symbols, standards records, and evaluation frames for signed-language writing.

slides.title: What This Series Reviews
slides.summary: Writing, notation, encoding, standards, and signed-language writing proposals can be reviewed through a shared layer-based method.

image_prompts.concrete: Critical review overview slide showing literature, notation systems, encoding proposals, standards records, and SignWriting evaluated through layers
image_prompts.abstract: A set of reviewed sources passing through a structured evaluation frame

slides.speech: This is the review series. It evaluates literature, theses, proposals, standards records, and recurring claims about signed-language writing through a shared layer-based method.

slides.title: Method
slides.summary: The review method separates writing, notation, encoding, rendering, infrastructure, evidence, and community authorship.

image_prompts.concrete: Method slide with layers labeled writing, notation, encoding, rendering, infrastructure, evidence, and community authorship
image_prompts.abstract: A review lens separating overlapping layers

bullets: Name what each source is actually evaluating
Credit strengths as well as limits
slides.speech: The method is to separate layers before judging claims. A source may be strong on notation and weak on writing. It may solve rendering without solving encoding. The review series tries to credit strengths, name limits, and make critique constructive.
slides.notes: This slide should make the series feel fair.

slides.title: Reading Paths
slides.summary: Readers can enter through the method, internal baseline, core external reviews, broader theory, or construction priorities.

image_prompts.concrete: Review reading map with paths for method, baseline, core literature, broader theory, and construction priorities
image_prompts.abstract: Multiple scholarly paths through a review field

slides.speech: The series has several valid paths. New readers can start with the overview. Method readers can go to the unified critique framework. Literature readers can enter through the external reviews. Readers looking for next steps can go to the construction-priorities entry.
slides.notes: Make the large review series feel navigable.

slides.title: Review Commitments
slides.summary: The series credits real strengths, names limits, separates layers, and avoids all-or-nothing judgments.

image_prompts.concrete: Scholarly commitments slide showing balanced scales for crediting strengths, naming limits, separating layers, and avoiding all-or-nothing judgments
image_prompts.abstract: Four steady review principles arranged as a fair evaluation compass

bullets: Use review to support better construction
slides.speech: The review commitments are simple. Credit real strengths before critique. Name limits without turning the source into a caricature. Separate layers before judging claims. Use review to support better writing systems, tools, standards, and research.
slides.notes: This completes the overview by naming the series posture. It should make the reviews feel fair, disciplined, and useful.
